title: [Submission] Warmdown Scheduling - 1.2430 BPB on 8×H100 SXM
body:
## Submission: Optimal LR Warmdown Scheduling

**Score:** 1.2430 BPB  
**Hardware:** 8×H100 SXM, 10 minutes  
**Author:** Majdi Zamim (@MajdiZamim)

## Key Insight

The default `WARMDOWN_ITERS=1200` never fires when training is 
wallclock-capped at 600 seconds on slower hardware, causing the 
model to terminate without proper LR decay.

By setting `WARMDOWN_ITERS=3000`, the cosine warmdown fires in 
the final ~30% of training steps, producing better convergence.

## Command
```bash
NCCL_IB_DISABLE=1 \
RUN_ID=submission_final \
VOCAB_SIZE=1024 \
NUM_LAYERS=9 \
MODEL_DIM=512 \
NUM_HEADS=8 \
NUM_KV_HEADS=4 \
TRAIN_BATCH_TOKENS=524288 \
MAX_WALLCLOCK_SECONDS=600 \
VAL_LOSS_EVERY=200 \
WARMDOWN_ITERS=3000 \
torchrun --standalone --nproc_per_node=8 train_gpt.py
```

## Files
- `README.md` — approach description
- `submission.json` — metadata
- `train.log` — full training log
- `train_gpt.py` — training script

comments.body: Thanks for the submission to the challenge! Given recent updates to the leaderboard this doesn't exceed the current SOTA. Note: PRs are all reviewed chronologically to keep things fair, given others can see future results. Consider trying to rebase your change on top of the current SOTA?

Updated submission with improved score: 1.1708 BPB (previous: 1.2430 BPB)

Key changes:
- NCCL_IB_DISABLE=0 (InfiniBand fix: 56ms → 49ms/step)
- 10 layers (up from 9)
- Spectral embedding init (overtone)
- Phase-transition resid_mix
- Muon weight decay 0.02
- FP16 tied embedding export
- Sliding window eval (EVAL_STRIDE=64)
- WARMDOWN_ITERS=3200 (tuned for our step count)
